reasoning: The Boston Celtics are currently the betting favorites to win the 2025 NBA Championship, with odds consistently hovering around +255 to +260 across major sportsbooks in late March 2025. This yields an implied probability of approximately 28%; after accounting for sportsbook vigorish, the true probability sits between 23% and 25%. Although they are likely to finish as the #2 seed behind the Cleveland Cavaliers (costing them potential home-court advantage in the Eastern Conference Finals), they possess a stellar 54-19 record and have significant postseason experience. Recent injuries have created some uncertainty—most notably, Kristaps Porzingis missed time with illness and shoulder issues, and Jayson Tatum suffered an ankle sprain on March 24. However, reports indicate Tatum's sprain is minor ("just sore," avoiding serious injury), and Porzingis has recently returned to form. The Celtics are resting and managing their starters' health ahead of the playoffs since their seeding is essentially locked. Because the core roster is intact and major injuries seem to have been avoided, the market odds remain an accurate reflection of their true chances. I align with the vig-adjusted odds at ~24%.
